figures.caption: FIG. 2. (a) Topological phase diagram characterized by Chern numbers in the θ-A parameter plane for an swave/AM heterojunction at JAM = 0.245. The red dashed line indicates A = 0.8. As θ varies along this dashed line, the system undergoes phase transitions between two distinct strong FCTSC phases and a topologically trivial phase. (b) Topological phase diagram in the θ-A parameter plane for an s-wave/AM heterojunction at JAM = 0.26. As A varies along the dashed line, the system evolves from a weak TSC state into an FCTSC state, and ultimately transitions into a topologically trivial state. The gray regions represent the topologically trivial phase. The yellow and green regions correspond to strong TSC states with Chern numbers N = ±1, while the blue regions indicate a weak topological superconducting state with a Chern number of N = 0.
FIG. 1. Schematic of the light-driven 2D AM-SC heterostructure, where the red and blue arrows denote spinup and spin-down, respectively.

FIG. 4. Topological phases in an AM/s + d-wave SC system. (a) Spin-resolved band structures along the high-symmetry path Y-Γ-X in the static limit (Ax = 0, JAM = 0.3). (b) Illustration of the distribution of bulk nodes in the Brillouin zone. (c)-(f) Topological edge state spectra of the AM/s+ dwave SC heterostructure along the surface Brillouin zone path Ȳ-Γ̄-Ȳ, driven by an EPL with a varying light amplitude Ax and exchange strength JAM. The specific parameters and resulting phases are: (c) N = −1 with JAM = 0.6 and Ax = 0.98; (d) N = −2 with JAM = 0.4 and Ax = 1.05; (e) HighChern-number state N = −3 with JAM = 0.4 and Ax = 0.7; and (f) N = −4 with JAM = 0.4 and Ax = 0.90. Other model parameters are fixed at µ = 0, λR = 0.3, and t = 1 for all plots.
